title: sudo does not work in foreign x86_64 containers under BuildKit + Rosetta 2
body:
I am running podman 5.3.1 on an Apple Silicon Mac, so x86_64 is a foreign architecture.

Consider a Dockerfile that creates a sudoer and then runs `sudo` as that user:

```
% cat Dockerfile 
FROM alpine
RUN apk add shadow sudo
RUN echo '%wheel ALL=(ALL:ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL' >> /etc/sudoers
RUN useradd --create-home --non-unique --uid 1000 --groups wheel user

USER 1000
RUN sudo /bin/ls
```

Building the container with BuildKit for a foreign architecture that is emulated by QEMU succeeds:

```
% docker buildx build --platform=linux/riscv64 . --load
...
```

But when it is run for x86_64 it fails:

```
% docker buildx build --platform=linux/x86_64 . --load
...                                                                                                                
 > [5/5] RUN sudo /bin/ls:
#0 0.066 sudo: effective uid is not 0, is /usr/bin/sudo on a file system with the 'nosuid' option set or an NFS file system without root privileges?

ERROR: failed to solve: process "/dev/.buildkit_qemu_emulator /bin/sh -c sudo /bin/ls" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
```

This command does *not* fail on Docker Desktop for Mac. Building with `podman build --platform=linux/x86_64 .` also works fine. The issue only affects Podman + BuildKit.


The `moby/buildkit` container image provides its own `buildkit-qemu-x86_64` binary but on Docker Desktop it *seems* that actually Rosetta 2 is used for x86_64 -- the container won't build if Rosetta isn't installed. But I do note that `/dev/.buildkit_qemu_emulator` in the intermediate build container does match BuildKit's `/usr/bin/buildkit-qemu-x86_64`.

I suspect Podman is somehow also using Rosetta; the VM provides `/proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/rosetta` not `/proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/qemu-x86_64-static`. (I didn't test without Rosetta installed.)

This may be related to https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/20445 but the solution is not the same. I think the binfmt_misc flags are correctly set nowadays:

```
% podman machine ssh cat /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/rosetta
enabled
interpreter /mnt/rosetta
flags: OCF
offset 0
magic 7f454c4602010100000000000000000002003e00
mask fffffffffffefe00fffffffffffffffffeffffff
```

comments.body:
I disabled the Rosetta 2 integration (https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24648) and subsequently the `docker buildx build --platform=linux/x86_64 . --load` command succeeded.

So in summary:

* It works with Docker Desktop + BuildKit + Rosetta 2.
* It works with Podman + BuildKit + QEMU.
* It works with Podman + Buildah + Rosetta 2.
* It fails with Podman + BuildKit + Rosetta 2.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I have experienced the same issue described here.
My environment is running a linux/x86_64 image on an OSX M1 (Apple Silicon) machine. In my case, the root user inside the container does have the necessary permissions to use the sudo command, but normal user does not.
I would like to know if there are any plans to address or improve this situation.
